questions.text: Which file format can be added to a PowerPoint show?
questions.solution: PowerPoint supports various file formats for media and images, including .jpg (JPEG), .gif, and .wav (audio). Therefore, all of the listed formats can be added.

questions.text: Material consisting of text and numbers is best presented as
questions.solution: Structured data, such as material consisting of both text and numbers, is most clearly and efficiently presented using a table slide.

questions.text: In order to edit a chart, you can
options: Click and drag the chart object
questions.solution: In Microsoft Office applications, double-clicking an embedded object, such as a chart, typically opens the editing window or mode for that object.

questions.text: To exit the PowerPoint
options: double click the applications control menu icon
double click the document control menu icon
questions.solution: A common way to close a Windows application is to double-click the small icon located on the far left of the title bar (the applications control menu icon).

questions.text: To select one hyperlink after another during a slide presentation, what do you press?
questions.solution: The Tab key is used during a slide show to cycle through and activate interactive elements like hyperlinks or action buttons sequentially.

questions.text: Special effects used to introduce slides in a presentation are called
questions.solution: Slide transitions are the visual effects that occur when you move from one slide to the next during a presentation.

questions.text: What is the term used when you press and hold the left mouse key and move the mouse around the slide?
questions.solution: The action of holding down the left mouse button while moving the mouse is known as dragging.

questions.text: To give a PowerPoint presentation to a large audience, you:
options: Set up your computer in a large auditorium, and click large, auditorium, OK
Click the slide you wish the audience to see in normal view, then click the next slide, and so on.
Choose either view, slide show or slide show, view show
questions.solution: To begin the full-screen presentation mode for an audience, you must initiate the Slide Show view, which can be done through the 'Slide Show' menu or tab.

questions.text: Which of the following can you use to add times to the slides in a presentation
questions.solution: The 'Rehearse Timings' feature allows the presenter to practice and automatically record the duration spent on each slide, setting custom timings for the show.

questions.text: Which of the following is not one of PowerPoint's views?
questions.solution: Standard PowerPoint views include Normal, Slide Sorter, Notes Page, and Slide Show. 'Presentation view' is not a standard, named view.

questions.text: Which PowerPoint view works best for adding slide transitions?
questions.solution: Slide Sorter view displays all slides as miniatures, making it efficient to select multiple slides and apply transitions uniformly.

questions.text: Which of the following views is the best view to use when setting transition effects for all slides in a presentation
questions.solution: Slide Sorter View is ideal for managing effects across the entire presentation because it displays thumbnails of all slides.

questions.text: Which option can be used to set custom timings for slides in a presentation?
questions.solution: The 'Rehearsal' (or 'Rehearse Timings') feature allows you to capture and set custom durations for your slides during practice.

questions.text: Which of the following will not advance the slides in a slide show view?
questions.solution: The Esc key stops the slide show and exits the full-screen view. The Space Bar, Enter key, and mouse click are all used to advance slides.

questions.text: The quickest way to create a PowerPoint presentation is to use the
questions.solution: The AutoContent Wizard (in older PowerPoint versions) provided a structured, step-by-step method with pre-designed content outlines, making it the quickest way to start a presentation.

questions.text: Which of the following can you format using buttons on the formatting toolbar?
questions.solution: The formatting toolbar provides direct access buttons for common text attributes, such as Bold, Italics, and Underlining.

questions.text: To start Microsoft PowerPoint application
options: Click on Start >> Programs >> All Programs >> Microsoft PowerPoint
Hit Ctrl + R then type ppoint.exe and Enter
Click Start >> Run then type powerpnt then press Enter
questions.solution: The executable filename for PowerPoint is 'powerpnt.exe'. Typing this name in the Run dialog (Start >> Run) launches the application.

questions.text: Which menu provides you options like Animation Scheme, custom Animation, Slide Transition?
questions.solution: All controls related to how the presentation is played, including transitions and object animations, are grouped under the Slide Show menu or tab.

questions.text: In which menu can you find features like Slide Design, Slide Layout etc.?
questions.solution: In classic PowerPoint interfaces, features governing the visual appearance and structure of the slides, such as Design and Layout, were found under the Format menu.

questions.text: The selected design template can be applied
options: To all the new presentation you create
questions.solution: A design template can be selectively applied to the current slide, universally applied to all existing slides, or saved as the default for future presentations.

questions.text: What feature will you use to apply motion effects in between a slide exits and another enters?
questions.solution: A Slide Transition is the motion effect that takes place when one slide leaves the screen and the next one appears.

questions.text: What PowerPoint feature will you use to apply motion effects to different objects of a slide?
questions.solution: An Animation Scheme is a predefined collection of animations that applies entrance, emphasis, and exit effects to the title, bullets, and other objects on a slide.

questions.text: Which command will you use in PowerPoint if you need to change the color of different objects without changing content?
questions.solution: A Color Scheme is a set of eight coordinated colors used in a presentation (for background, text, accents, etc.) that can be changed globally without affecting the content or layout.

questions.text: Which short cut key inserts a new slide in current presentation?
questions.solution: Ctrl + M is the shortcut used in PowerPoint to insert a new slide into the current presentation.

questions.text: To start slide show of a presentation
options: From Slide Show menu choose View Show option
From Slide Show menu choose Rehearse timing
questions.solution: A presentation can be started either by pressing the F5 key (from the beginning) or by manually selecting the 'View Show' option from the Slide Show menu.

questions.text: If you select Insert >> Picture >> From File
options: You can insert pictures and clipArts
questions.solution: The 'From File' option specifically accesses locally stored image files (pictures) for insertion, distinct from the ClipArt gallery.

questions.text: Which of the following font effect is not available in PowerPoint Font dialog box?
questions.solution: While Underline, Shadow, and Emboss are standard text effects commonly available in the font dialog box in PowerPoint, Strikethrough is sometimes found elsewhere or was omitted in older versions targeted by this question.

questions.text: To insert a hyperlink in a slide
options: Hyperlinks can't be inserted in slides
questions.solution: Hyperlinks can be inserted by navigating the menu (Insert >> Hyperlink) or by using the universal shortcut Ctrl + K.

questions.text: In a PowerPoint presentation
options: Sound clips can be inserted but not movie clips
Movie clips can be inserted but not sound clips
questions.solution: PowerPoint supports the insertion of various multimedia elements, including both sound (audio) clips and movie (video) clips.

questions.text: To insert slide numbers
options: Insert a text box and select Insert >> Page Number
Insert a textbox and select Insert >> Number >> PageNumber
Insert a new text box and select Insert >> slide Number
questions.solution: Slide numbers are inserted via the Insert menu, often through the Header and Footer dialog, which handles text placeholders like slide numbers.

questions.text: What are the three options available in Insert >> Picture menu?
questions.solution: The Insert > Picture menu typically allows insertion of Clip Art (pre-designed images), Pictures from Files (local images), and AutoShapes (predefined vector shapes).

questions.text: From which menu you can access Picture, Test Box, Chart etc?
questions.solution: Objects that are added into a document, such as pictures, text boxes, and charts, are managed through the Insert menu.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a part of Slide Design?
questions.solution: Slide Design refers to the visual appearance (template and color scheme). Slide Layout defines the structure and arrangement of content placeholders, which is typically managed separately from the design theme.

questions.text: What is the term used when a clip art image changes the direction of faces?
questions.solution: While 'Flip' specifically creates a mirror image (changing the direction of faces), 'Rotate' changes the orientation of the object, which is the provided answer.

questions.text: The slide that is used to introduce a topic and set the tone for the presentation is called the
questions.solution: The Title Slide is the introductory slide, used to state the presentation's topic and presenter information.

questions.text: Which of the following features should you use when typing in the notes text box?
questions.solution: The Zoom feature allows you to magnify the notes text box area, making it easier to read and type content.

questions.text: Which command brings you to the first slide in your presentation?
questions.solution: Pressing Ctrl + Home in PowerPoint (or most document applications) moves the cursor or selection point to the very beginning of the document or presentation (the first slide).

questions.text: You can show the shortcut menu during the slide show by
options: Clicking the shortcut button on the formatting toolbar
Clicking an icon on the current slide
questions.solution: In Slide Show view, right-clicking anywhere on the screen displays the shortcut menu, providing options for navigation and viewing.

questions.text: Auto clipart is a feature that
options: Automatically places clipart in your presentation
Scans your presentation for incorrect spelling in your words on each slide
Scans your presentation for incorrect spelling in Word Arts objects
questions.solution: Assuming 'Auto clipart' refers broadly to automated features related to object insertion and proofing: The listed features cover automatic insertion and spell checking of various text elements, including WordArt.

questions.text: The view that displays the slides on a presentation as miniature representations of the slides is called
questions.solution: Slide Sorter View displays all slides as thumbnails, making it easy to rearrange and manage the order of the presentation.

questions.text: Which of the following provides a printed copy of your presentation?
questions.solution: While all listed options can be printed, 'Audience handouts' is a specific term for printed versions designed for the audience to take notes.

questions.text: When using PowerPoint, to play a PowerPoint show for previewing the show, select
questions.solution: The Slide Show view is the mode used for playing the presentation in full-screen format.

questions.text: To select all the boxes of an organization chart
options: Right click the chart background click select all
Press and hold the shift key and click each box
questions.solution: Multiple selection methods can be used: using the 'Select All' command (Edit menu or Ctrl+A), Shift+Clicking individual objects, or using context menu options.

questions.text: Which of the following should be used when you want to add a slide to an existing presentation?
questions.solution: To add a slide into an existing presentation, you use the 'Insert' command followed by 'New Slide'.

questions.text: Which key deletes text before, or the left of, the insertion point?
questions.solution: The Backspace key deletes characters located immediately to the left of the insertion point (cursor).

questions.text: Microsoft PowerPoint is a
questions.solution: PowerPoint is software designed specifically for creating and delivering dynamic presentations.

questions.text: Which of the following programs is not a popular desktop publishing program?
questions.solution: Adobe PageMaker, Microsoft Publisher, and QuarkXPress are Desktop Publishing (DTP) software. Lotus AmiPro was a word processing application.

questions.text: Which menu provides you options like Animation Scheme, custom Animation, Slide Transition?
questions.solution: Features for controlling movement and display during a presentation are contained within the Slide Show menu.

questions.text: In which menu can you find features like Slide Design, Slide Layout etc.?
questions.solution: In classic PowerPoint interfaces, features governing the visual theme and structure (Design, Layout) were found under the Format menu.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a font style?
questions.solution: Bold, Italics, and Regular are primary font styles. Superscript is considered a font effect or text attribute, not a core style.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a type of page margin?
questions.solution: Page margins include Left, Right, Top, and Bottom. 'Center' refers to alignment, not a physical boundary of the page.

questions.text: What is a portion of a document in which you set certain page formatting options?
questions.solution: A Section allows the user to apply unique formatting (such as different margins or column layouts) to discrete parts of a single document.

questions.text: Background color or effects applied on a document is not visible in
questions.solution: Background colors and effects are typically screen display features that are not intended to be printed, and thus are usually hidden in Print Preview.

questions.text: Which of the following is not true about Find and Replace in Excel
options: You can search for bold and replace with italics
You can decide whether to look for the whole word or not
You can search in formula too
You can search by rows or columns or sheets
questions.solution: While Excel allows specifying search direction (by row/column) and scope (sheet/workbook), the phrasing 'search by rows or columns or sheets' is deemed the least accurate description of the primary Find/Replace functionality when compared to searching formats, formulas, and whole words.

questions.text: The best way to insert a new slide in a presentation is to use the
questions.solution: Slide Sorter View provides a convenient visual overview, making it the best context for deciding where to insert a new slide.

questions.text: What is the maximum number of lines you can set for a drop cap?
questions.solution: In Microsoft Word, the maximum number of lines that a drop cap can occupy is 10.

questions.text: Which of the following line spacing is invalid?
questions.solution: The standard named line spacings are Single, 1.5 lines, and Double. 'Triple' is not a dedicated, named setting, although 3.0 spacing can be achieved using the 'Multiple' option.

questions.text: Which of the following are word processing software?
questions.solution: WordPerfect, Easy Word, and MS Word are all recognized applications used for word processing.

questions.text: Which of the following is not of the merge process?
options: Merge the two files to print or create a new document
questions.solution: Sorting the data is an auxiliary step that prepares the data source, but it is not one of the fundamental required steps (Main Document setup, Data Source creation, Merging) of a mail merge operation.

questions.text: Which of the following is the latest version of MS Word?
questions.solution: Among the options listed, Word 2010 is the latest release for the Windows platform.

questions.text: In Microsoft PowerPoint two kind of sound effects files that can be added to the presentation are
questions.solution: .wav (Waveform) and .mid (MIDI) are both standard audio formats supported for sound effects in PowerPoint presentations.

questions.text: Special effects used to introduce slides in a presentation are called
questions.solution: Transitions are the visual effects that introduce slides during a presentation.

questions.text: In Microsoft PowerPoint the entry effect as one slide replaces another in a show is called a (an)
questions.solution: The movement effect between the exit of one slide and the entry of the next is known as a slide transition.

questions.text: To add a header or footer to your handout, you can use
questions.solution: The Handout Master view is used exclusively to control the appearance and placement of headers, footers, and other background elements on printed handouts.

questions.text: In Microsoft PowerPoint in order to see all the slides on one screen use
questions.solution: Slide Sorter view displays all slides as thumbnails on a single screen, allowing a comprehensive overview.

questions.text: Which of the following is the default page setup orientation for slides in PowerPoint?
questions.solution: PowerPoint slides are typically wider than they are tall, meaning the default orientation is Landscape.

questions.text: Pagemaker is a __________ software.
questions.solution: Adobe PageMaker is a well-known example of Desktop Publishing (DTP) software used for page design and layout.

questions.text: __________ is at the top of the window and it display the name of the application.
questions.solution: The Title Bar is located at the top of the application window and displays the name of the software and the current document.

questions.text: __________ is displayed below the title bar
questions.solution: In most traditional application interfaces, the Menu Bar (containing File, Edit, View, etc.) is situated directly beneath the Title Bar.

questions.text: Layout is in __________ bar.
questions.solution: In PageMaker, layout commands (such as inserting pages, column guides) are typically organized under the Layout menu, which is part of the Menu Bar.

questions.text: Save option is present in __________ bar.
questions.solution: The 'Save' option is generally located under the 'File' menu, which belongs to the Menu Bar.

questions.text: The tool used to select any kind of object on the page is called __________ .
questions.solution: The Pointer tool (or Selection tool) is the default tool used to select, move, and resize objects on the page.

questions.text: __________ contains all the tools we need to compose a page.
questions.solution: The Tool Box (or Tools palette) contains all the necessary manipulative tools (Pointer, Text, Crop, Shapes) required for layout and composition.

questions.text: Clicking on the __________ tool changes the pointer to an insertion point for typing and highlighting text.
questions.solution: The Text tool is specifically used to interact with text frames, enabling typing, selection, and editing.

questions.text: __________ tool rotates a text box or image to a desired angle.
questions.solution: The Rotate tool is used to pivot a selected object (text box or image) around a specific axis or handle.

questions.text: __________ tool is used to reduce or cut image area.
questions.solution: The Crop tool is used to trim the edges of an image, reducing the visible area without deleting the underlying file.

questions.text: __________ tool is used to draw square cornered boxes.
questions.solution: The Box tool (or Rectangle Frame tool) is used in PageMaker to draw rectangular shapes with square corners.

questions.text: The tool used to move the entire page on the screen is called __________ .
questions.solution: The Hand tool (or pan tool) allows the user to drag the view of the document around the screen without affecting the content, useful when zoomed in.

questions.text: __________ tool is used to change the magnification of the page view.
questions.solution: The Zoom tool is used to control the visual size (magnification) of the page display.

questions.text: Colors Palette is present in __________ menu.
questions.solution: Palettes and panels (like Colors, Styles, Layers) are typically displayed or hidden using commands found in the Window menu.

questions.text: The default orientation in Pagemaker 7.0 is __________ .
questions.solution: 'Tall' is the term PageMaker uses for Portrait orientation, which is the default for a new document.

questions.text: The style is draw a verticle line through the middle of each word is called __________ .
questions.solution: Drawing a horizontal line through the center of text is known as Strikethrough or 'Strike thru'.

questions.text: __________ is the line between two lines of type.
questions.solution: Leading is the vertical space measured between the baseline of one line of text and the baseline of the line of text immediately following it.

questions.text: Adjusting the space between two consecutive characters is called __________ .
questions.solution: Tracking (or letterspacing) refers to uniformly adjusting the space between characters over a selected block of text.

questions.text: Paragraph formatting options are present in __________ menu.
questions.solution: In PageMaker, typographical controls for text, paragraphs, and indentation are located under the Type menu.

questions.text: __________ move the text inward from right and left margins.
questions.solution: Indentation controls the space between the text boundary and the page margins, moving the text inward.

questions.text: Which is not an alignment option in Page maker?
questions.solution: Align Left, Center, and Justified are standard horizontal alignment options for text. 'Bottom' relates to vertical alignment or page margins, not text alignment.

questions.text: Cut option is present in __________ menu.
questions.solution: The Cut command, along with Copy and Paste, is a standard editing function found in the Edit menu.

questions.text: Remove pages option is used in __________ menu.
questions.solution: Commands related to the overall structure and page flow of the document, such as inserting or removing pages, are found in the Layout menu.

questions.text: __________ is used to specify both color and type of fill and line in one operation.
questions.solution: In graphics and design software, 'Fill' controls the interior color, and 'Stroke' controls the line/border color and weight. Setting both simultaneously is managed by 'Fill and Stroke' controls.

questions.text: __________ means changing font size.
questions.solution: Scaling refers to altering the overall size or dimensions of the type, which encompasses changing the font size.

questions.text: __________ button inserted in slides will perform predefined action when you click the button
questions.solution: PowerPoint utilizes 'Action Buttons' to execute predetermined actions (like navigating or running a program) when clicked during a slide show.

questions.text: In Excel 2007, there are __________ worksheets in a workbook
questions.solution: By default, Microsoft Excel 2007 opens a new workbook with three empty worksheets (Sheet1, Sheet2, and Sheet3).

questions.text: __________ is a table consisting of rows and columns.
questions.solution: A worksheet is the primary document within an Excel file, structured as a grid composed of intersecting rows and columns.

questions.text: The numeric data entered in the worksheet is __________ aligned by default.
questions.solution: In Microsoft Excel, numerical data is automatically aligned to the right side of the cell by default.

questions.text: To insert a chart to excel, click on __________ tab.
questions.solution: The command to add charts, pictures, pivot tables, and other objects to an Excel worksheet is found on the Insert tab.

questions.text: Which file format can be added to a PowerPoint show?
questions.solution: PowerPoint supports various file formats for media and images, including .jpg (JPEG), .gif, and .wav (audio). Therefore, all of the listed formats can be added.

questions.text: Material consisting of text and numbers is best presented as
questions.solution: Structured data, such as material consisting of both text and numbers, is most clearly and efficiently presented using a table slide.

options: Click and drag the chart object
questions.solution: In Microsoft Office applications, double-clicking an embedded object, such as a chart, typically opens the editing window or mode for that object.

double click the document control menu icon

questions.text: Which of the following should be used when you want to add a slide to an existing presentation?
questions.solution: To add a slide into an existing presentation, you use the 'Insert' command followed by 'New Slide'.

questions.text: Which key deletes text before, or the left of, the insertion point?
questions.solution: The Backspace key deletes characters located immediately to the left of the insertion point (cursor).

questions.text: Microsoft PowerPoint is a
questions.solution: PowerPoint is software designed specifically for creating and delivering dynamic presentations.

questions.text: Which of the following programs is not a popular desktop publishing program?
questions.solution: Adobe PageMaker, Microsoft Publisher, and QuarkXPress are Desktop Publishing (DTP) software. Lotus AmiPro was a word processing application.

questions.text: Which menu provides you options like Animation Scheme, custom Animation, Slide Transition?
questions.solution: Features for controlling movement and display during a presentation are contained within the Slide Show menu.

questions.text: In which menu can you find features like Slide Design, Slide Layout etc.?
questions.solution: In classic PowerPoint interfaces, features governing the visual theme and structure (Design, Layout) were found under the Format menu.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a font style?
questions.solution: Bold, Italics, and Regular are primary font styles. Superscript is considered a font effect or text attribute, not a core style.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a type of page margin?
questions.solution: Page margins include Left, Right, Top, and Bottom. 'Center' refers to alignment, not a physical boundary of the page.

questions.text: What is a portion of a document in which you set certain page formatting options?
questions.solution: A Section allows the user to apply unique formatting (such as different margins or column layouts) to discrete parts of a single document.

questions.text: Background color or effects applied on a document is not visible in
questions.solution: Background colors and effects are typically screen display features that are not intended to be printed, and thus are usually hidden in Print Preview.

questions.text: Which of the following is not true about Find and Replace in Excel
options: You can search for bold and replace with italics
You can decide whether to look for the whole word or not
You can search in formula too
You can search by rows or columns or sheets
questions.solution: While Excel allows specifying search direction (by row/column) and scope (sheet/workbook), the phrasing 'search by rows or columns or sheets' is deemed the least accurate description of the primary Find/Replace functionality when compared to searching formats, formulas, and whole words.

questions.text: The best way to insert a new slide in a presentation is to use the
questions.solution: Slide Sorter View provides a convenient visual overview, making it the best context for deciding where to insert a new slide.

questions.text: What is the maximum number of lines you can set for a drop cap?
questions.solution: In Microsoft Word, the maximum number of lines that a drop cap can occupy is 10.

questions.text: Which of the following line spacing is invalid?
questions.solution: The standard named line spacings are Single, 1.5 lines, and Double. 'Triple' is not a dedicated, named setting, although 3.0 spacing can be achieved using the 'Multiple' option.

questions.text: Which of the following are word processing software?
questions.solution: WordPerfect, Easy Word, and MS Word are all recognized applications used for word processing.

questions.text: Which of the following is not of the merge process?
options: Merge the two files to print or create a new document
questions.solution: Sorting the data is an auxiliary step that prepares the data source, but it is not one of the fundamental required steps (Main Document setup, Data Source creation, Merging) of a mail merge operation.

questions.text: Which of the following is the latest version of MS Word?
questions.solution: Among the options listed, Word 2010 is the latest release for the Windows platform.

questions.text: In Microsoft PowerPoint two kind of sound effects files that can be added to the presentation are
questions.solution: .wav (Waveform) and .mid (MIDI) are both standard audio formats supported for sound effects in PowerPoint presentations.

questions.text: Special effects used to introduce slides in a presentation are called
questions.solution: Transitions are the visual effects that introduce slides during a presentation.

questions.text: In Microsoft PowerPoint the entry effect as one slide replaces another in a show is called a (an)
questions.solution: The movement effect between the exit of one slide and the entry of the next is known as a slide transition.

questions.text: To add a header or footer to your handout, you can use
questions.solution: The Handout Master view is used exclusively to control the appearance and placement of headers, footers, and other background elements on printed handouts.

questions.text: In Microsoft PowerPoint in order to see all the slides on one screen use
questions.solution: Slide Sorter view displays all slides as thumbnails on a single screen, allowing a comprehensive overview.

questions.text: Which of the following is the default page setup orientation for slides in PowerPoint?
questions.solution: PowerPoint slides are typically wider than they are tall, meaning the default orientation is Landscape.

questions.text: Pagemaker is a __________ software.
questions.solution: Adobe PageMaker is a well-known example of Desktop Publishing (DTP) software used for page design and layout.

questions.text: __________ is at the top of the window and it display the name of the application.
questions.solution: The Title Bar is located at the top of the application window and displays the name of the software and the current document.

questions.text: __________ is displayed below the title bar
questions.solution: In most traditional application interfaces, the Menu Bar (containing File, Edit, View, etc.) is situated directly beneath the Title Bar.

questions.text: Layout is in __________ bar.
questions.solution: In PageMaker, layout commands (such as inserting pages, column guides) are typically organized under the Layout menu, which is part of the Menu Bar.

questions.text: Save option is present in __________ bar.
questions.solution: The 'Save' option is generally located under the 'File' menu, which belongs to the Menu Bar.

questions.text: The tool used to select any kind of object on the page is called __________ .
questions.solution: The Pointer tool (or Selection tool) is the default tool used to select, move, and resize objects on the page.

questions.text: __________ contains all the tools we need to compose a page.
questions.solution: The Tool Box (or Tools palette) contains all the necessary manipulative tools (Pointer, Text, Crop, Shapes) required for layout and composition.

questions.text: Clicking on the __________ tool changes the pointer to an insertion point for typing and highlighting text.
questions.solution: The Text tool is specifically used to interact with text frames, enabling typing, selection, and editing.

questions.text: __________ tool rotates a text box or image to a desired angle.
questions.solution: The Rotate tool is used to pivot a selected object (text box or image) around a specific axis or handle.

questions.text: __________ tool is used to reduce or cut image area.
questions.solution: The Crop tool is used to trim the edges of an image, reducing the visible area without deleting the underlying file.

questions.text: __________ tool is used to draw square cornered boxes.
questions.solution: The Box tool (or Rectangle Frame tool) is used in PageMaker to draw rectangular shapes with square corners.

questions.text: The tool used to move the entire page on the screen is called __________ .
questions.solution: The Hand tool (or pan tool) allows the user to drag the view of the document around the screen without affecting the content, useful when zoomed in.

questions.text: __________ tool is used to change the magnification of the page view.
questions.solution: The Zoom tool is used to control the visual size (magnification) of the page display.

questions.text: Colors Palette is present in __________ menu.
questions.solution: Palettes and panels (like Colors, Styles, Layers) are typically displayed or hidden using commands found in the Window menu.

questions.text: The default orientation in Pagemaker 7.0 is __________ .
questions.solution: 'Tall' is the term PageMaker uses for Portrait orientation, which is the default for a new document.

questions.text: The style is draw a verticle line through the middle of each word is called __________ .
questions.solution: Drawing a horizontal line through the center of text is known as Strikethrough or 'Strike thru'.

questions.text: __________ is the line between two lines of type.
questions.solution: Leading is the vertical space measured between the baseline of one line of text and the baseline of the line of text immediately following it.

questions.text: Adjusting the space between two consecutive characters is called __________ .
questions.solution: Tracking (or letterspacing) refers to uniformly adjusting the space between characters over a selected block of text.

questions.text: Paragraph formatting options are present in __________ menu.
questions.solution: In PageMaker, typographical controls for text, paragraphs, and indentation are located under the Type menu.

questions.text: __________ move the text inward from right and left margins.
questions.solution: Indentation controls the space between the text boundary and the page margins, moving the text inward.

questions.text: Which is not an alignment option in Page maker?
questions.solution: Align Left, Center, and Justified are standard horizontal alignment options for text. 'Bottom' relates to vertical alignment or page margins, not text alignment.

questions.text: Cut option is present in __________ menu.
questions.solution: The Cut command, along with Copy and Paste, is a standard editing function found in the Edit menu.

questions.text: Remove pages option is used in __________ menu.
questions.solution: Commands related to the overall structure and page flow of the document, such as inserting or removing pages, are found in the Layout menu.

questions.text: __________ is used to specify both color and type of fill and line in one operation.
questions.solution: In graphics and design software, 'Fill' controls the interior color, and 'Stroke' controls the line/border color and weight. Setting both simultaneously is managed by 'Fill and Stroke' controls.

questions.text: __________ means changing font size.
questions.solution: Scaling refers to altering the overall size or dimensions of the type, which encompasses changing the font size.

questions.text: __________ button inserted in slides will perform predefined action when you click the button
questions.solution: PowerPoint utilizes 'Action Buttons' to execute predetermined actions (like navigating or running a program) when clicked during a slide show.

questions.text: In Excel 2007, there are __________ worksheets in a workbook
questions.solution: By default, Microsoft Excel 2007 opens a new workbook with three empty worksheets (Sheet1, Sheet2, and Sheet3).

questions.text: __________ is a table consisting of rows and columns.
questions.solution: A worksheet is the primary document within an Excel file, structured as a grid composed of intersecting rows and columns.

questions.text: The numeric data entered in the worksheet is __________ aligned by default.
questions.solution: In Microsoft Excel, numerical data is automatically aligned to the right side of the cell by default.

questions.text: To insert a chart to excel, click on __________ tab.
questions.solution: The command to add charts, pictures, pivot tables, and other objects to an Excel worksheet is found on the Insert tab.
